No Poverty.reason: The course provides programming skills, enhancing access to new technology and economic resources, indirectly supporting Target 1.4 by improving opportunities for all.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with indispensable programming skills for employment and problem-solving (Target 4.4).
evidence: 程式設計儼然成為各行業不可或缺的基本能力

Gender Equality.reason: The course teaches information technology and programming, which are enabling technologies that can promote empowerment for all, aligning with Target 5.b.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to water, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly teaches programming as an 'indispensable basic skill' for 'all walks of life,' directly supporting technological upgrading and decent work (Target 8.2).
evidence: 程式設計儼然成為各行業不可或缺的基本能力
處理資料進而解決問題

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers information technology, data science, and web data, directly upgrading technological capabilities and fostering innovation (Targets 9.5, 9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By designing project content for students from 'different fields,' the course indirectly promotes economic inclusion and reduces skill-based inequalities (Target 10.2).
evidence: 將針對不同領域的學生設計不同內容的專題內容

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to sustainable consumption, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Explicitly stating 'Respect Copyright' supports the rule of law (Target 16.3). Data science skills also facilitate public access to information (Target 16.10).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course teaches data science and visualization, crucial for statistical capacity-building and monitoring SDG progress (Targets 17.18, 17.19).
